content:
The Codacy MCP tool is returning `Unauthorized` — the API token isn't configured in this session. You'll need to set `CODACY_API_TOKEN` in your environment for the MCP server to authenticate.

Can you run:

```
! echo $CODACY_API_TOKEN
```

If it's empty, you'll need to add it. You can get a token from the Codacy dashboard under **Your account → Access management → API tokens**, then:

```
! export CODACY_API_TOKEN=<your-token>
```

Once the token is in the environment, restart Claude Code (or the MCP server) so the Codacy tools can pick it up, and I can pull the cloud tool configuration and sync it with `.codacy/codacy.config.json` and `.codacy.yml`.

Done. Here's what changed and why:

**`codacy.config.json` updates:**

| Tool | Before | After | What was added |
|---|---|---|---|
| ESLint9 | 25 patterns | 133 patterns | All `sonarjs/*` (19), `security/*` (14), `security-node/*` (18), `@typescript-eslint/*` (23 total, was 5), `xss`, `vue`, `eslint-plugin` |
| Lizard | missing | 4 patterns | Complexity thresholds: CCN, NLOC, file NLOC, parameter count |
| Semgrep | 10 patterns | 10 patterns (unchanged) | Functionally equivalent to cloud's Opengrep; same rule format |
| Trivy | 4 patterns | 4 patterns (unchanged) | Already in sync |

The biggest gap closed is the security rules — the cloud was flagging things like `sonarjs/no-hardcoded-secrets`, `security-node/detect-nosql-injection`, `@typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises` that would never appear in a local scan before. Now your local run will surface the same issues a PR will.
